::::: {.cover-container} ::: {.thesis-title style="text-align: left; margin-bottom: 1.5rem;"}

/REF References

:::

The City of the Captive Globe

:::: {.columns style="text-align: left;"} ::: {.column width="48%"} - Portugali, J. (2016). What Makes Cities Complex? In J. Portugali & E. Stolk (Eds.), *Complexity, Cognition, Urban Planning and Design* (pp. 3–19). Springer International Publishing. - Stolk, E., & Portugali, J. (2016). A Complexity-Cognitive View on Scale in Urban Design. In J. Portugali & E. Stolk (Eds.), *Complexity, Cognition, Urban Planning and Design* (pp. 217–252). Springer International Publishing. - Kant, I. (1998). *Critique of pure reason* (P. Guyer & A. W. Wood, Trans.). Cambridge University Press. (Original work published 1781) - Fondation Gabriel Péri. (2022, April 21). *Éric Puisais, Henri Lefebvre, une philosophie de la géographie* [Video]. YouTube. - Koolhaas, R. (1995). Bigness, or the Problem of Large. In R. Koolhaas & B. Mau (Eds.), *S,M,L,XL* (pp. 495–516). The Monacelli Press. - Lefebvre, H. (1972). *Urbanose: Entretien avec Henri Lefebvre* [Documentary]. Office National du Film du Canada. - De Roo, G. (2016). Framing the Planning Game: A Cognitive Understanding of the Planner’s Rationale in a Differentiated World. In J. Portugali & E. Stolk (Eds.), *Complexity, Cognition, Urban Planning and Design* (pp. 173–199). Springer International Publishing. - Kousoulas, S., & Radman, A. (2026). Annotate This! Semiotisation, Automation and the Recursive Causality of Images. In A. Biglari (Ed.), *Open Semiotics* (Vol. 5). - Koolhaas, R. (2002). Junkspace. *October*, 100, 175–190. - von Uexküll, J. (1957). A stroll through the worlds of animals and men: A picture book of invisible worlds (C. H. Schiller, Trans.). In C. H. Schiller (Ed.), *Instinctive behavior: The development of a modern concept* (pp. 5–80). International Universities Press. (Original work published 1934) - Aristotle. (1984). *Metaphysics* (W. D. Ross, Trans.). In J. Barnes (Ed.), *The complete works of Aristotle: The revised Oxford translation* (Vol. 2, pp. 1552–1728). Princeton University Press. (Original work published ca. 350 B.C.E.) - Heidegger, M. (1977). The age of the world picture. In W. Lovitt (Trans.), *The question concerning technology and other essays* (pp. 115–154). Harper & Row. (Original work published 1950) - Simondon, G. (2017). *On the mode of existence of technical objects* (C. Malaspina & J. Rogove, Trans.). Univocal Publishing. (Original work published 1958) - Sloterdijk, P. (2016). *Foams: Spheres volume III: Plural spherology* (W. Hoban, Trans.). Semiotext(e). (Original work published 2004). - Le Corbusier. (1923). *Vers une architecture*. G. Crès et Cie. - Fabian Alvarado David. (2016, 7 juillet). *Le mouvement dans les sociétés hypermodernes François Ascher* [Vidéo]. YouTube. - Capurro, R. (2003). *Angeletics - A message theory*. Rafael Capurro. - Coccia, E. (2021). *Filosofia della casa: Lo spazio domestico e la felicità*. Einaudi. ::: ::: {.column width="4%"} ::: ::: {.column width="48%"} ### Image Citations - Uhl, Johannes H.: Animating 200 years of urban spatial development in 35 U.S. cities. Copernicus Publications, 2020. - United Nations, Department of Economic and Social Affairs, Population Division. (2019). *World urbanization prospects* - Zhu, X. X., Chen, S., Zhang, F., Shi, Y., and Wang, Y.: GlobalBuildingAtlas: an open global and complete dataset of building polygons, heights and LoD1 3D models, Earth Syst. Sci. Data, 17, 6647–6668, 2025. - von Uexküll, J. (1957). A stroll through the worlds of animals and men: A picture book of invisible worlds. In C. H. Schiller (Ed. & Trans.), *Instinctive behavior: The development of a modern concept* (pp. 5–80). International Universities Press. (Original work published 1934) - *Blind men and the elephant* [Illustration]. (1907). In *The Heath readers by grades* (p. 69). D.C. Heath and Company. - Stolk, E., & Portugali, J. (2016). A Complexity-Cognitive View on Scale in Urban Design. In J. Portugali & E. Stolk (Eds.), *Complexity, Cognition, Urban Planning and Design* (pp. 217–252). Springer International Publishing. - Eisen, C. (1755). *Frontispiece* [Engraving]. In M.-A. Laugier, *Essai sur l'architecture* (2nd ed.). Duchesne. - Le Corbusier. (1914). *Maison Dom-Ino, elevation/perspective view* [Architectural Drawing]. Fondation Le Corbusier, Paris, France. - Levittown library. (1950). Early Capes Aerial View of Levittown. - SITE (1981), *Highrise of houses*. - Sharpsteen, B. (Director). (1938). *Mickey's trailer* [Film]. Walt Disney Productions. - Ed Ruscha (1966), *Standard Station* - Shuttle Nasa Topography Mission (SRTM) (1999) - Google Earth. (2018, December 7). *Google Earth Studio - Animation Reel* [Video]. YouTube. - Biljecki, F., Ledoux, H., & Stoter, J. (2016). An improved LOD specification for 3D building models. *Computers, Environment and Urban Systems*, 59, 25–37. - Rem Koolhaas & Madelon Vriesendorp, *The City of the Captive Globe*, New York, 1972. Gouache and pencil on paper, 318 × 441 mm. The Museum of Modern Art, New York. - NVIDIA. (2025, March 24). *NVIDIA Omniverse Foundational Technology Montage I GTC Spring 2025 Edition* [Video]. YouTube. - Currens Ventosa, Oostvoorne NL 1993, photo: Adriaan Kok - Ron Herron *Walking City on the Ocean*, project (Exterior perspective) 1966 ::: :::: :::::
